summary: Agent bootstrap for using Proxima ai_docs immediately.
Performance and representation invariants for Proxima work.
host-a M1 full bench matrix for prime/tokio/hyper/pingora comparisons.
Human architecture overview for Proxima concepts, runtime model, and feature flags.

North-star constraints for PTY, process-containment, VM mirror, and adjacent Proxima component work: RISC reuse, teaching surface, tier discipline, config parity, source polymorphism, evidence gates, generated constraints, and licensing. NOTE: the original ../pty-tester/docs/proxima-pty/guiding-principles.md no longer exists in this checkout (the pty-tester tree retains only discipline.md). The principles are distributed across docs/proxima-quic/guiding-principles.md, docs/proxima-h3/guiding-principles.md, and per-component discipline.md rows pending consolidation into one workspace doc.
Evidence and design correction for contained discovery, seal, and replay of an opaque hardened-runtime process.
Verified local feasibility and missing implementation layers for running the exact macOS target binary in a Proxima-owned Apple VM boundary.
Bare-metal scratch VM Pipe ground proven on Apple Hypervisor.framework and Linux KVM with trapped hello-world output.
A working-system mirror is a first-class Pipe whose VM is internal and whose admitted terminal, file, network, and lifecycle surfaces are composed, dynamically replaceable resource pipes.

Spec-complete greenfield rewrite of proxima-quic + proxima-http::http3 (RFC 9000/9001/9002/9114/9204/9221/9297 + multipath draft). Sans-IO protocol modules proxima-protocols::quic + proxima-protocols::http3_codec now live inside proxima-protocols (folded from the former proxima-quic-proto/proxima-h3-proto crates); proxima-quic remains its own facade crate, proxima-h3 folded into proxima-http::http3. Tier-1 sans-IO + tier-3 alloc-free leaves + runtime-agnostic facade. Both `native` and `quinn-compat` Cargo features ship indefinitely (no atomic cutover); tokio remains a direct dep of the native facade today because the UDP accept loop uses tokio::net, with a prime Datagram reactor source tracked as the path to tokio-free. Plan slug: i-need-a-full-adaptive-seal.

Sans-IO QUIC + TLS 1.3 protocol crate per RFC 9000/9001/9002/9221 + multipath draft. Tier-1 (no_std + alloc); leaf modules tier-3 (no_std + no_alloc). Publicly consumable outside proxima. A0 scaffolding 2026-05-24.
Sans-IO HTTP/3 + QPACK protocol crate per RFC 9114/9204/9220/9297. Tier-1 (no_std + alloc); leaf modules tier-3 (no_std + no_alloc). Publicly consumable outside proxima. A0 scaffolding 2026-05-24.

Future-shaped Endpoint + Connection facade over proxima-protocols::quic (the `native` feature) AND a quinn-backed shim (the `quinn-compat` feature). Both ship together; consumers pick per build. The native path is quinn-free (gate-enforced by `proxima-http quinn-free (native feature only)` cell) but still depends on tokio because the UDP accept loop uses tokio::net + tokio::spawn — the prime Datagram reactor source is the planned path off tokio. The `TOKIO_FREE_FACADE_ENFORCE` gate cell is staged but not on by default.

Future-shaped H3 server + client facade over proxima-protocols::http3_codec + proxima-quic (the `http3-native` feature) AND h3 + h3-quinn (the `http3-quinn-compat` feature); now lives at proxima-http::http3. Both ship together; the listener picks at runtime per the spec. Native path includes the per-connection driver, the listener with O(1) demux + random SCID, and per-stream backpressure retention. Tokio is required on the native path today (listener UDP loop).

prime async TCP connect + proxima-net::prime (PrimeTcpUpstream: StreamUpstream, folded from the former proxima-net-prime crate) — disciplined-component log; keystone for the consumer relay prime migration. algorithm-development runs first (connect state machine: EINPROGRESS->Interest::Write->SO_ERROR + lost-wakeup correctness). Incumbent: tokio connect; expected parity (connect is per-connection, handshake-RTT-bound) — value is capability not speed.

Prime reactor/read wakeup discipline log. Current finding: readiness epochs moved cached empty `poll_read -> Pending` to ~20.7 ns, and internal trace found/fixed stale kqueue connect-write filters causing repeated empty reactor turns. Post-fix Prime reaches first reactor turn in ~0.31 us, has one turn/read with zero stale/empty turns, and local ready/pending read probes are tied with or faster than Tokio. Remaining blocking echo RTT comparisons are host-scheduler sensitive, not a proven Prime local-path loss.

PostgreSQL wire reintroduction discovery: upstream pgwire 0.28 server-api is tokio-coupled and allocation-heavy; Proxima needs a sans-IO codec crate plus a prime/proxima stream facade instead of embedding pgwire. TLS is existing substrate via proxima-tls, not new pgwire-owned protocol work. The target is full PostgreSQL wire capability; phases are sequencing, not scope reduction.

PostgreSQL wire support: proxima-protocols::pgwire_codec (sans-IO no_std no-alloc message codec + session FSM, full protocol matrix incl. messages upstream pgwire omits, folded from the former proxima-pgwire-codec crate) + proxima-pgwire facade (futures-io driver, ListenProtocol mount, TLS via SSLRequest, trust/cleartext auth). Capability matrix, gate rows, bench scorecard, named gates G1-G8 in the discipline log.
